evidence: As part of our broader digital transformation strategy, we are integrating artificial intelligence to support our internal business functions and exploring additional uses for the future.
In recent years, we have increased our investment in technology and automation enabling us to operate more efficiently and better serve our customers.
We are integrating artificial intelligence to support our internal business functions and exploring additional uses for the future.
AI presents risks and challenges and may result in unintended consequences, including producing inaccurate data.

AI algorithms and training methodologies may be flawed... AI-related legal and regulatory landscape is evolving... could entail significant costs or limit our ability to incorporate certain AI capabilities.
AI-related issues, deficiencies and failures could also give rise to legal or regulatory action... damage our reputation or otherwise materially harm our business.
The emergence of artificial intelligence and its role in accelerating cybersecurity attacks will only make this area of risk more dynamic and difficult to prevent.

limitations: No AI-attributable dollar amounts or quantified investment figures found in excerpts; capex_mentions list is empty.
AI investment described only in generic terms ('integrating AI to support internal business functions') with no named programs, products, or dollar amounts, limiting investment intensity score to 2.
AI narrative is aspirational and exploratory; no named deployed use cases or quantified outcomes disclosed in excerpts.
Capital expenditure figures cited (~1% of net sales) are tied to general technology and automation, not specifically AI-attributable per the excerpts.
